comments: .review/findings/live-dial-tuning-plan.md:6 — Status still ends "owner Draft→Active approval pending" (and :94 "until the owner approves Draft→Active"). This is a live review record: .review/README.md says findings/ records remain live, the reviewloop playbook flips a finding's Status per verdict, and REVIEW.md:4 sends readers here for per-finding details. After D-2026-07-16-2 the review records therefore still name owner approval — not ldt-1 — as the pending next step. Update the Status line to record activation (D-2026-07-16-2; ldt-1 next).

docs/plan/ONE_TRANSFER_PATH.md:102-103 — The ACTIVE plan that docs/STATE.md:133 says governs all work still reads "Draft `docs/plan/LIVE_DIAL_TUNING.md` owns the reviewed correction". Draft means no-implementation in this repo (docs/agent/PROTOCOL.md plan step 6), and PROTOCOL.md's decision step 2 requires editing text a decision renders stale now — "Do not leave stale text standing". Precedent: at the prior activation (3a87ba0, D-2026-07-05-4) no live doc still called the activated plan Draft; only frozen .review/results snapshots kept old wording. Flip the word to Active (D-2026-07-16-2); the plan-§6 drift warning itself correctly stays until the ldt-2/3 cutover.

docs/TRANSFER_SESSION.md:65 — The Active session contract still says LIVE_DIAL_TUNING.md "is the Draft correction". Same class of defect: a cold agent following .agents/repo-guidance.md's reading order (STATE → active plan docs → REVIEW/.review → contracts) meets records disagreeing about whether activation happened, and AGENTS.md's flag-conflicts invariant forces it to stop and file drift instead of cleanly starting ldt-1 — precisely the ambiguity the goal set out to remove. These three cheap fixes are why the verdict is reopened rather than accepted under the best-way question.

docs/STATE.md:3,5,23,51,136,157; REVIEW.md:18-27; docs/DECISIONS.md:274-286; DEVLOG.md:7; docs/plan/LIVE_DIAL_TUNING.md:3,8-9,371 — Verified correct and mutually consistent at the primary layer: STATE (199 lines, within the 200 cap) says NEXT ACTION — IMPLEMENT ldt-1 with no residual live-dial Draft/pending language; ldt-1 is the topmost open `[ ]` row in REVIEW.md and the ldt-1..4 row titles match the plan's §Slices; D-2026-07-16-2 appears exactly once, in house format, recording the owner's "go"; the DEVLOG activation entry is latest-first at the top; the plan's Status/Decision ref/Supersedes/Open questions are all flipped. git diff 8f08546..acd368f touches exactly the five documentation files — no code/proto/runtime/.review change, matching the commit's safety claims. Documentation-only is the right mechanism for this goal (activation is an owner-state fact, not a code change); the miss is the unswept secondary references above.

guard proof — 26 semantic assertions (plan Active + unique decision record + STATE/REVIEW/DEVLOG identify ldt-1 + topmost-open-row check + docs-only five-file diff shape + scripts/agent/check-docs.sh) all green at acd368f; git-restoring all five files to 8f08546 turned exactly the 20 activation assertions red while check-docs.sh alone stayed green (the repo gate cannot detect de-activation — the semantic assertions are the discriminator); restoring only docs/plan/LIVE_DIAL_TUNING.md to base turned exactly the 6 plan-scoped assertions red with all record assertions green (guard localizes); exact reviewed bytes then restored via git restore --source=acd368f — git status --porcelain empty, git diff-index --quiet acd368f passes, HEAD detached at acd368f338089a32e8d810fcecd4f580f572816a.

.review/check-state.sh:121 — pre-existing condition, not a finding against this change: the lint reports 43 historical verified+reopened verdict pairs; the reviewed commit touches nothing under .review/ (per the diff file list), so the state is identical at base by construction. Noted so it is not attributed to the activation commit; excluded from the guard in favor of the repo's actual docs gate.
